text:
is the 19th chapter of the 2nd volume and the 46th chapter overall of the Spoof on Titan manga, written and illustrated by hounori.

Overview

 Dimo and Flegel Reeves take turns trying to convince people to buy their products. 

Summary

At the market, Dimo convinces a crowd to purchase his newest item, a knife with a blade that can cut through anything with ease. As he demonstrates the knife's usage, Eren comments that there must be something that it cannot cut and that Dimo cannot convince him to buy it. However, Dimo claims that the knife can cut through a Titan's skin, which immediately interests Eren. Mikasa stops him from buying the knife.

Some time later, Dimo sells a block of Yarckel cheese. He offers the crowd samples. Sasha, being present in the crowd, keeps asking for more samples to satisfy her hunger, reasoning that she has yet to reach a decision with each request. She eventually asks Dimo for a bread sample to pair the cheese with.

Dimo convinces an old woman to buy perfume to reignite her husband's desire for her. Once her husband comes home, she asks if he feels anything toward her as the scent wafts through the air. He tells her that he does not.

Dimo lets Flegel take over the business for a day, calling it business training. He watches Flegel sell tea leaves. Levi unexpectedly takes one of the cups of tea, which makes Flegel mad. Dimo scolds him for his behavior, so he tries to approach Levi more nicely and continues his marketing scheme. When Flegel asks Levi unnecessary questions, Dimo worries that he will potentially sabotage their marketing strategy. After tasting the tea, he concludes that he cannot choose a winner and walks away. Dimo scolds Flegel once again.

Flegel gives Historia a brooch for free after finding her cute. Hearing this, Dimo is in disbelief. Later on, Dimo finds Historia and Ymir out together, with Ymir wearing a familiar butterfly brooch on her shirt.

Mikasa asks if Eren is okay after the latter was convinced to buy one of Dimo's knives. He claims that it is impossible to defeat a Titan with a chef's knife and that he was almost duped into believing it could. However, Dimo suddenly introduces a newer knife, similar to the old one, for no extra charge. Eren becomes convinced that he can now take down a Titan with the two knives on sale.
